sections.content:
The RIGHT action moved the player. Now I need to check where the player moved to and build a pathfinding solution. Let me check the new player position and then use BFS to find the path from player to target.
The player was at boundary [[15,15, 15], [15, 17], [16, 16], [17, 16], [17, 17], [17, 15]]

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

player_id = None
target_id = None
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'b': player_id = n['id']
    if n['color'] == 'N': target_id = n['id']

player_node = nodes[player_id]
target_node = nodes[target_id]

pb = player_node['boundary']
tb = target_node['boundary']

print("Player boundary:", pb)
print("Target boundary:", tb)

# Get all white path cells to build the maze grid
# Parse ascii to find walkable cells
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
rows = len(lines)
cols = len(lines[0]) if lines else 0

# Find all walkable cells (white, blue, green) and wall (gray) cells
grid = []
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    row = []
    for j, ch in enumerate(line):
        if ch == 'W': row.append(0)  # path
        elif ch == 'b': row.append(0)  # player
        elif ch == 'N': row.append(0)  # target
        else: row.append(1)  # wall/obstacle
    grid.append(row)

# BFS from player to target
from collections import deque
start = None
end = None
for i in range(rows):
    for j in range(cols):
        if grid[i][j] == 0:
            if i == rows-1 and j == cols-1: continue  # skip if out of bounds
            if grid[i][j] == 0:
                start = (i, j)
            if grid[i][j] == 0 and grid[i][j] == 0:
                end = (i, j)

if start and end:
    queue = deque([(start, [])])
    visited = set([start])
    while queue:
        (r, c), path = queue.popleft()
        if (r, c) == end:
            print("Path found:", path)
            # Convert path to actions
            actions = []
            for move in path:
                actions.append(move)
            print("Actions:", actions)
            action(actions)
            break
        for dr, dc, move in [(-1, 0, 'UP'), (1, 0, 'DOWN'), (0, -1, 'LEFT'), (0, 1, 'RIGHT')]:
            nr, nc = r+dr, c+dc
            if 0 <= nr < rows and 0 <= nc < cols and grid[nr][nc] == 0 and (nr, nc) not in visited:
                visited.add((nr, nc))
                queue.append(((nr, nc), path + [move]))
else:
    print("No path found")
</parameter>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
rows = len(lines)
cols = len(lines[0])

start = None
end = None
for i in range(rows):
    for j in range(cols):
        if j < len(lines[i]):
            ch = lines[i][j]
            if ch == 'b': start = (i, j)
            if ch == 'N': end = (i, j)

print("Start:", start)
print("End:", end)

from collections import deque
grid = []
for i in range(rows):
    row = []
    for j in range(cols):
        ch = lines[i][j]
        if ch in 'WbNB':
            row.append(0)
        else:
            row.append(1)
    grid.append(row)

queue = deque([(start, [])])
visited = set([start])
found = False
while queue:
    pos, path = queue.popleft()
    r, c = pos
    if r == end[0] and c == end[1]:
        print("Path:", path)
        action(path)
        found = True
        break
    for dr, dc, move in [(-1, 0, 'UP'), (1, 0, 'DOWN'), (0, -1, 'LEFT'), (0, 1, 'RIGHT')]:
        nr, nc = r+dr, c+dc
        if 0 <= nr < rows and 0 <= nc < cols and grid[nr][nc] == 0 and (nr, nc) not in visited:
            visited.add((nr, nc))
            queue.append(((nr, nc), path + [move]))

if not found:
    print("No path found")
</parameter>

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

player_id = None
target_id = None
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'b': player_id = n['id']
    if n['color'] == 'N': target_id = n['id']

player_node = nodes[player_id]
target_node = nodes[target_id]

pb = player_node['boundary']
tb = target_node['boundary']

print("Player boundary:", pb)
print("Target boundary:", tb)

# Get all white path cells to build the maze grid
# Parse ascii to find walkable cells
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
rows = len(lines)
cols = len(lines[0]) if lines else 0

# Find all walkable cells (white, blue, green) and wall (gray) cells
grid = []
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    row = []
    for j, ch in enumerate(line):
        if ch == 'W': row.append(0)  # path
        elif ch == 'b': row.append(0)  # player
        elif ch == 'N': row.append(0)  # target
        else: row.append(1)  # wall/obstacle
    grid.append(row)

# BFS from player to target
from collections import deque
start = None
end = None
for i in range(rows):
    for j in range(cols):
        if grid[i][j] == 0:
            if i == rows-1 and j == cols-1: continue  # skip if out of bounds
            if grid[i][j] == 0:
                start = (i, j)
            if grid[i][j] == 0 and grid[i][j] == 0:
                end = (i, j)

if start and end:
    queue = deque([(start, [])])
    visited = set([start])
    while queue:
        (r, c), path = queue.popleft()
        if (r, c) == end:
            print("Path found:", path)
            # Convert path to actions
            actions = []
            for move in path:
                actions.append(move)
            print("Actions:", actions)
            action(actions)
            break
        for dr, dc, move in [(-1, 0, 'UP'), (1, 0, 'DOWN'), (0, -1, 'LEFT'), (0, 1, 'RIGHT')]:
            nr, nc = r+dr, c+dc
            if 0 <= nr < rows and 0 <= nc < cols and grid[nr][nc] == 0 and (nr, nc) not in visited:
                visited.add((nr, nc))
                queue.append(((nr, nc), path + [move]))
else:
    print("No path found")

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
rows = len(lines)
cols = len(lines[0])

start = None
end = None
for i in range(rows):
    for j in range(cols):
        if j < len(lines[i]):
            ch = lines[i][j]
            if ch == 'b': start = (i, j)
            if ch == 'N': end = (i, j)

print("Start:", start)
print("End:", end)

from collections import deque
grid = []
for i in range(rows):
    row = []
    for j in range(cols):
        ch = lines[i][j]
        if ch in 'WbNB':
            row.append(0)
        else:
            row.append(1)
    grid.append(row)

queue = deque([(start, [])])
visited = set([start])
found = False
while queue:
    pos, path = queue.popleft()
    r, c = pos
    if r == end[0] and c == end[1]:
        print("Path:", path)
        action(path)
        found = True
        break
    for dr, dc, move in [(-1, 0, 'UP'), (1, 0, 'DOWN'), (0, -1, 'LEFT'), (0, 1, 'RIGHT')]:
        nr, nc = r+dr, c+dc
        if 0 <= nr < rows and 0 <= nc < cols and grid[nr][nc] == 0 and (nr, nc) not in visited:
            visited.add((nr, nc))
            queue.append(((nr, nc), path + [move]))

if not found:
    print("No path found")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"action_semantics": "direction keys move player VERIFIED(step 1)", "object_taxonomy": "blue=player, green=target, gray=wall, white=path", "hud_map": "bottom bar is timer", "win_pattern": "reach green", "level_log": [], "cross_level_notes": "maze navigation", "world_model": "maze with player and goal", "goal_model": "navigate from blue to green", "recent_findings": "RIGHT moves player right VERIFIED(step 1)", "open_questions": "need to build full path", "current_plan": "BFS from player to target", "failed_probes": []}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
